blurbs.title: Varigotti — Baia dei Saraceni
blurbs.body: Pastel houses on the cliff, dark sand, the Gallipoli analog Angela's profile points to. Smaller bay, often calm in the morning. Polpo Ubriaco trattoria 200 m back from the sand.

blurbs.title: Noli
blurbs.body: The smallest of Italy's four maritime republics, with the Romanesque San Paragorio still standing. Gambero rosso di Noli at Ponte Antico — Slow Food Presidium prawn from Noli's own small fleet, found nowhere else.

blurbs.title: Spotorno
blurbs.body: Long sand strip between Noli and Bergeggi, tamer surf than Varigotti. The lungomare runs flat for a 2-km walk; D.H. Lawrence wintered here in 1925-26.

blurbs.title: Bergeggi
blurbs.body: Protected marine area facing the small Isola di Bergeggi. Snorkeling and free-diving register; dive shops in Spotorno run the boat over for €25-35. Skip the lungomare, drive to the headland viewpoint above SS1.

blurbs.title: Punta Crena (Varigotti west)
blurbs.body: The Ruffino family's Vigneti Punta Crena clings to the cliff above the cove between Varigotti and Noli — terraced Pigato and Lumassina, a tasting room booked direct. Book ahead; the cellar door is a working farm, not a visitor centre.

blurbs.title: Albenga
blurbs.body: Pre-Roman city with three medieval towers still standing in the centro storico, the 5th-century baptistery, and Liguria's only intact Roman amphitheatre. The artichoke-and-purple-asparagus plain begins here.

blurbs.title: Alassio
blurbs.body: The British Riviera in miniature — Hemingway and Edward Lear both wintered here. Muretto signed by celebrities on Via Dante; the Budello, a 1.5-km medieval pedestrian spine running the length of the old town. Crowded in July; manageable in early June.

blurbs.title: Laigueglia
blurbs.body: Five minutes south of Alassio and almost no day-tripper traffic. Pastel old town, calmer beach, 1500s parish church. The walk-up trattoria register at Baia del Sole for a focaccia-and-fritto-misto lunch under €30 per cover.
